title: excoecaria macrocarpa
intro: Excoecaria macrocarpa, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its toxic sap and medicinal properties. The sap of the plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Excoecaria macrocarpa include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and relieving pain. Excoecaria macrocarpa is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve for external use, and its medicinal properties have been studied for potential applications in modern medicine.
